title: APPENDIX — Quick-reference summary (not for reading aloud)

lines.text: APPENDIX — Quick-reference summary (not for reading aloud)
Y-shaped, four chains (two heavy, two light), two identical Fab arms (variable, antigen-binding), one Fc stem (constant, effector-recruiting).
Six CDR loops at the tip of each arm form the actual binding surface.
V(D)J recombination = combinatorial shuffling of gene segments in the bone marrow, plus sloppy junctional joining = ~10^11 possible sequences in the naive repertoire.
Then somatic hypermutation in germinal centers, with selection for affinity = affinity maturation.
Affinity = strength of one arm's grip (KD).
Avidity = combined strength when both/all arms grip.
IgG (workhorse, blood, placenta-crossing, most therapeutic mAbs).
Bone marrow → V(D)J → tolerance check → naive → activated by antigen+T-cell help → either plasmablast or germinal center → long-lived plasma cell (factory) or memory B cell (reserve).

1890s: horse antitoxin (polyclonal, foreign, serum sickness) 1975: hybridomas (Köhler & Milstein) — first monoclonals, but mouse 1980s-90s: chimeric → humanized antibodies (CDR grafting) 1990s onward: phage display (Smith, Winter), transgenic mice → fully human antibodies

Tag-and-kill (rituximab, anti-CD20 on B cell lymphoma) Block-the-driver (trastuzumab, anti-HER2 on breast cancer) Neutralize-the-messenger (infliximab/adalimumab, anti-TNF in autoimmunity) Release-the-brake (checkpoint inhibitors: anti-CTLA-4, anti-PD-1, anti-PD-L1) Guided missile (ADCs: antibody + linker + cytotoxic payload; e.g. trastuzumab emtansine) Double-sided sticky tape (bispecifics, BiTEs; e.g. blinatumomab brings T cells to CD19+ leukemia)
Fab (one arm, no Fc) → scFv (just the variable domains, single chain) → nanobody (camelid single-domain, ~1/10 the size of full IgG).
Half-life extension via FcRn-binding mutations; effector-silent or effector-enhanced Fcs; glycoengineering (afucosylation) for stronger NK-cell killing.
CAR-T (antibody-derived recognition fused to T cell signaling, redirecting T cells to attack chosen targets).
